gaps.title: 278 event implementation ownership is too broad for downstream parallel work.
gaps.resolution: 281 publishes the explicit event-owner override registry and treats the 278 implementationOwnerTask field as non-authoritative for parallel launch.

gaps.title: 280 gap ownership uses generic track labels for waitlist and fallback seams.
gaps.resolution: 281 maps exact owners for waitlist runtime, assisted booking, and reconciliation.

gaps.title: Live provider activation and evidence capture remain outside the first local-booking wave.
gaps.resolution: Keep provider onboarding deferred and simulator-backed until the dedicated activation wave starts.

gaps.title: Frontend booking routes cannot switch to live fetch until backend runtime tracks exist.
gaps.resolution: 281 blocks the frontend wave behind 282 to 292 rather than letting the shell compose local fake truth.
